howItWorks.intro: This surface area calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is surface area?
faq.answer: Surface area is the total area of all the surfaces (faces) of a three-dimensional shape. Imagine unfolding a box flat — the surface area is the total area of that flat shape (called a net).

faq.question: How do you find the surface area of a rectangular prism?
faq.answer: Add the areas of all 6 faces: SA = 2(lw + wh + lh). A rectangular prism has 3 pairs of identical faces.

faq.question: What is the difference between surface area and volume?
faq.answer: Surface area measures the total area of the outside surfaces (in square units), while volume measures the space inside (in cubic units). Think of wrapping paper (surface area) vs. filling a box (volume).

faq.question: How do you find the surface area of a cylinder?
faq.answer: A cylinder has two circular bases and one rectangular side (when unrolled). SA = 2πrh (side) + 2πr² (two bases).
